schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Equatorial Wave–Force Dynamics and Spacetime Curvature: L_rm Eq

theory.canonical_statement: Building upon the analysis in \texttt{equatorial.txt} (:contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}), we represent the equatorial $\beta$-plane expansion as \begin{equation} f(\phi) = 2\Omega \sin\phi \approx \beta\, y, \qquad \beta = \frac{2\Omega}{R}, \qquad y = R(\phi - \pi/2), \end{equation} so that the shallow-water operator reduces to the parabolic-cylinder operator \begin{equation} \mathcal{L}_{\rm eq} = \partial_t^2 - c^2 \partial_x^2 - c^2 \partial_y^2 + \beta\,y\,\partial_x. \end{equation} The meridional structure function is a Hermite mode: \begin{equation} \Psi_n(y) = e^{-\beta y^2/(2c)} \,H_n\!\left(\sqrt{\frac{\beta}{c}}\,y\right), \end{equation} which simultaneously satisfies a geodesic-deviation analogue: \begin{equation} \frac{D^2\xi^\mu}{D\tau^2} + R^\mu{}_{\nu\rho\sigma}u^\nu\xi^\rho u^\sigma =0. \end{equation}
theory.technical_interpretation: Equatorial Wave–Force Dynamics and Spacetime Curvature: L_rm Eq is a lagrangian construction, written L_rm eq = ∂_t^2 - c^2 ∂_x^2 - c^2 ∂_y^2 + β y ∂_x.. It defines a Lagrangian density from which the dynamics follow by the principle of least action. It is built from β, c, x, y, defining L_rm eq. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
